touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Daniel Sato introducing AskElephant's call recording and coaching platform. No response received.
Attempted cold call to Daniel Sato. Left voicemail briefly introducing AskElephant and value proposition for sales coaching. No callback.
LinkedIn connection request and message sent to Daniel mentioning PropTech sales challenges and AskElephant's coaching tools. No response.
Brief cold call connected with Daniel. He expressed mild interest in call recording for his sales team but was rushed. Agreed to schedule a proper qualification call next week.

questions_asked.question: Are you currently recording and reviewing sales calls at PrismNetworks?
questions_asked.answer: Not systematically. We've talked about it but haven't implemented anything yet.

next_steps: Schedule 30-minute qualification call for next Tuesday

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Daniel covered current coaching process and team structure. He manages 9 reps and struggles with consistent coaching. Interested in automations but mentioned budget will be tight this quarter.

questions_asked.question: How are you coaching your team today without call recordings?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly ad-hoc feedback and pipeline reviews. I don't have visibility into actual conversations unless I'm shadowing.

questions_asked.question: What's your biggest pain point with the current approach?
questions_asked.answer: Inconsistency. Some reps are crushing it, others are struggling, and I can't pinpoint why without being on every call.

questions_asked.question: What does pricing typically look like for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: Depends on the plan, but for 9 seats with automation features, typically $9-12K annually. We can discuss options.

objections: Budget is going to be challenging this quarter, we're watching spend closely

touches.ai_summary: Demo call showcasing call recording, AI coaching insights, and automation features. Daniel was engaged and saw clear value for his team. Asked about implementation timeline and whether we could work with their limited budget. No other stakeholders joined the call.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation usually take?
questions_asked.answer: Most teams are up and running within a week. We provide onboarding support and training.

questions_asked.question: Can you work with us on price? Our budget is closer to $7K than $10K.
questions_asked.answer: Let me talk to my manager about what we can do. I'll come back with options.

questions_asked.question: Would it make sense to loop in your CFO or VP of Operations on the business case?
questions_asked.answer: I can handle the approval on my end. I have budget authority for tools under $10K.

objections: The standard pricing is above our budget allocation for this quarter
next_steps: Send custom proposal with pricing options
Follow up on budget approval timeline

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call focused on pricing and contract terms. Daniel pushed hard on price, citing budget constraints. Agreed to $7,100 ACV (27.5% discount) for 9 seats with automations plus consulting package. Daniel wants to close this week. No mention of involving other stakeholders for final approval.

questions_asked.question: If I can get approval this week, can you hold this price through Q2?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, if we close by Friday, we can lock this rate for your first year.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to sign off on this before we can move forward?
questions_asked.answer: Just me. I'll run it by my boss as a courtesy but I have the authority to approve.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a no-brainer for you to sign this week?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, if you can hit that $7,100 number and throw in the consulting hours, I'm ready to move.

objections: I really can't go above $7,100 without getting into budget battles internally
next_steps: Send final contract at $7,100 with automations_plus_consulting package
Target signature by end of week
